req.title: Break-glass access
req.statement: A clinician shall invoke break-glass access for an emergent patient; break-glass use shall be audited and trigger a post-event review.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-021 defines three verifiable behaviours: (1) a valid clinician can invoke break-glass and receive access plus an audit event, (2) a non-clinician (or unauthenticated caller) must be denied, and (3) the audit/review pipeline must handle edge cases such as the minimum-viable justification string. EP covers the happy-path partition; NEG covers the unauthorised-role partition; BVA covers the justification field length boundary; STC covers the full state transition from normal→break-glass→review-created; DT covers the combination of role × patient-existence conditions. Five tests are provided to satisfy the minimum-three requirement and give fuller coverage.

tests.name: test_break_glass_granted_for_valid_clinician
preconditions: Target patient record exists in fhir-lite
actions: POST /BreakGlass with valid clinician JWT, patient_id, and non-empty justification
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query audit log for the emitted event
expected: Audit event record exists with actor=clinician and event_type=break_glass
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_break_glass_granted_for_valid_clinician(
    clinician_auth_headers,
    existing_patient_id,
    api_client,
    audit_log_client,
):
    payload = {
        "patient_id": existing_patient_id,
        "justification": "Emergency trauma case requires immediate record access",
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/BreakGlass", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("access_granted") is True

    audit_event = audit_log_client.find_latest(
        actor_role="clinician",
        patient_id=existing_patient_id,
        event_type="break_glass",
    )
    assert audit_event is not None, "Audit event not emitted"
    assert audit_event["actor_role"] == "clinician"

    review_task = audit_log_client.find_review_task(
        patient_id=existing_patient_id,
        trigger_event_id=audit_event["id"],
    )
    assert review_task is not None, "Post-event review task not created"
    assert review_task["status"] in ("pending", "open")

tests.name: test_break_glass_denied_for_non_clinician_role
preconditions: Target patient record exists in fhir-lite
actions: POST /BreakGlass with admin-role JWT, patient_id, and non-empty justification
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error indicating insufficient role
No audit event emitted for break_glass
trace: R-HLT-021: unauthorised actors must not gain access
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_break_glass_denied_for_non_clinician_role(
    admin_auth_headers,
    existing_patient_id,
    api_client,
    audit_log_client,
):
    payload = {
        "patient_id": existing_patient_id,
        "justification": "Admin attempting break-glass",
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/BreakGlass", json=payload, headers=admin_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body, "Missing error description in response"

    spurious_event = audit_log_client.find_latest(
        actor_role="admin",
        patient_id=existing_patient_id,
        event_type="break_glass",
    )
    assert spurious_event is None, "Audit event must NOT be emitted for denied request"

tests.name: test_break_glass_justification_minimum_length_boundary
preconditions: Target patient record exists in fhir-lite
API enforces a minimum justification length (assumed: 1 character)
actions: POST /BreakGlass with a single-character justification (length = 1, at boundary)
POST /BreakGlass with empty justification (length = 0, below boundary)
expected: Single-character justification: HTTP 200 or 201, access_granted true
Empty justification: HTTP 400 or 422, access denied
trace: R-HLT-021: clinician asserts emergency (justification field boundary)
R-HLT-021: access granted only when preconditions met
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_break_glass_justification_minimum_length_boundary(
    clinician_auth_headers,
    existing_patient_id,
    api_client,
):
    # At boundary: length == 1
    resp_at = api_client.post(
        "/BreakGlass",
        json={"patient_id": existing_patient_id, "justification": "E"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp_at.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Single-char justification rejected: {resp_at.status_code}"
    )
    assert resp_at.json().get("access_granted") is True

    # Below boundary: length == 0
    resp_below = api_client.post(
        "/BreakGlass",
        json={"patient_id": existing_patient_id, "justification": ""},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp_below.status_code in (400, 422), (
        f"Empty justification should be rejected: {resp_below.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_break_glass_state_transition_normal_to_review
preconditions: Patient record is in NORMAL access state (no active break-glass session)
Audit log starts with no break_glass event for this patient
actions: Assert patient access state is NORMAL via GET /BreakGlass/{patient_id}/status
POST /BreakGlass to trigger break-glass (state: NORMAL -> BREAK_GLASS_ACTIVE)
Assert state eventually transitions to REVIEW_PENDING after event emission
expected: After POST: state = BREAK_GLASS_ACTIVE, access_granted = true
Audit event emitted with event_type = break_glass
Final state: REVIEW_PENDING with review task created
trace: R-HLT-021: break-glass use shall be audited
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_break_glass_state_transition_normal_to_review(
    clinician_auth_headers,
    fresh_patient_id,
    api_client,
    audit_log_client,
    poll_until,
):
    status_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/BreakGlass/{fresh_patient_id}/status",
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert status_resp.status_code == 200
    assert status_resp.json()["state"] == "NORMAL"

    bg_resp = api_client.post(
        "/BreakGlass",
        json={"patient_id": fresh_patient_id, "justification": "Cardiac arrest"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert bg_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert bg_resp.json()["access_granted"] is True

    active_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/BreakGlass/{fresh_patient_id}/status",
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert active_resp.json()["state"] == "BREAK_GLASS_ACTIVE"

    review_state = poll_until(
        lambda: api_client.get(
            f"/BreakGlass/{fresh_patient_id}/status",
            headers=clinician_auth_headers,
        ).json()["state"],
        expected="REVIEW_PENDING",
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert review_state == "REVIEW_PENDING"

tests.name: test_break_glass_decision_table_missing_patient
preconditions: Patient ID used does not exist in fhir-lite
actions: POST /BreakGlass with valid clinician JWT but non-existent patient_id
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-HLT-021: DT row: role=clinician, patient_exists=false -> denied
tests.executable:
import pytest

NONEXISTENT_PATIENT_ID = "patient-does-not-exist-00000"

def test_break_glass_decision_table_missing_patient(
    clinician_auth_headers,
    api_client,
    audit_log_client,
    ensure_patient_absent,
):
    ensure_patient_absent(NONEXISTENT_PATIENT_ID)

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/BreakGlass",
        json={
            "patient_id": NONEXISTENT_PATIENT_ID,
            "justification": "Emergency for unknown patient",
        },
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 404, f"Expected 404, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "patient" in str(body).lower(), "Response must reference patient lookup failure"

    spurious = audit_log_client.find_latest(
        patient_id=NONEXISTENT_PATIENT_ID,
        event_type="break_glass",
    )
    assert spurious is None, "No audit event should be created for non-existent patient"
